title: Segfault when running podman-remote cp
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

when running podman-remote with specific arguments it crashes

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `podman-remote  cp /tmp/file1 cont43:/tmp/ccc` where `cont43` is **local** container (not remote) and `/tmp/file1` is an arbitrary file.

**Describe the results you received:**
It crashes:
```
$ podman-remote  cp /tmp/compare_data cont43:/tmp/ccc
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0x556625ab0af9]

goroutine 1 [running]:
panic(0x556626221f40, 0x5566273aafe0)
        /usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/panic.go:565 +0x2c9 fp=0xc00052d6f8 sp=0xc00052d668 pc=0x556624ba3e09
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
This is actually not regular podman-remote usage, but still better not to crash.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.5.1
```

comments.body:
I think this is fixed upstream

@mheon right, checked now with upstream `podman version 1.6.2-dev` and there is no `podman-remote cp` at all. Is it planned to implement?
Closing the issue.
We are planning on adding it, but it's not been implemented yet.
@mheon cool! It would unblock option to run ansible with podman connection plugin on remote host, because it requires "cp", prepared for it in https://github.com/ansible/ansible/pull/63166
